reasons: Khufra can punish Sora's dash and leap-based engage before Sora freely reaches the backline.
Sora depends on mobility to enter, chase, and continue form-based pressure, while Khufra can disrupt that movement path with anti-blink control.
Khufra's frontline positioning forces Sora to commit through a control zone instead of freely choosing a clean target.

proof.summary: Sora wants to enter fights with mobility and continue pressure through Thunder or Torrent follow-up, while Khufra can punish blink-style movement and interrupt Sora's clean engage path.
worksBestWhen: Khufra saves his anti-dash control for Sora's real engage.
Khufra positions between Sora and the backline target Sora wants to reach.
The team is ready to punish Sora when his movement is interrupted.
failureCases: Sora waits for Khufra to use his control first.
Sora uses Thunder ultimate's untargetable timing to avoid the first control attempt.
Khufra is isolated and cannot protect the target Sora is diving.

reasons: Minsitthar can restrict Sora's mobility inside King’s Calling, making it harder for Sora to continue his close-range combo after committing.
Sora depends on repeated movement and form-based follow-up, while Minsitthar creates a teamfight zone that punishes directional mobility.
Minsitthar is especially valuable when Sora must dive into a grouped fight instead of taking an isolated duel.

proof.summary: Sora wants to enter, reposition, and keep fighting through form-based movement, while Minsitthar can create a zone that restricts directional mobility and makes Sora easier to punish after entry.
worksBestWhen: Minsitthar casts King’s Calling after Sora commits.
Sora is forced to fight inside the King’s Calling zone.
Minsitthar's team follows up while Sora's movement options are limited.
failureCases: Sora baits King’s Calling and disengages before committing.
Minsitthar casts too early or too far from Sora's real target.
Sora's team zones Minsitthar away before he can place the field.

reasons: Karrie directly punishes Sora's Torrent form because Torrent increases Sora's Max HP profile.
Karrie's Max HP true damage gives a reliable damage-profile answer when Sora becomes tankier instead of playing only for burst.
Karrie is strongest into Sora when she is protected and can keep attacking long enough to stack her true-damage mark.

proof.summary: Sora's Torrent form increases his tank-style Max HP profile, while Karrie's Lightwheel Mark deals True Damage based on the target's Max HP after stacking marks, giving Karrie a direct answer to Torrent-form Sora.
worksBestWhen: Sora is using Torrent form or building durable.
Karrie has frontline peel and enough time to stack Lightwheel Mark.
The fight is front-to-back instead of a clean Sora flank onto Karrie.
failureCases: Sora reaches Karrie before she can stack marks.
Sora uses Thunder burst style and avoids extended DPS trades.

reasons: Valentina can copy Sora's ultimate, which is central to Sora's Thunder and Torrent form identity.
Sora's ultimate is not only damage; it enables his form-based power spike, so Valentina can contest that same strategic tool during important fights.
Valentina's value depends on copying Sora at the correct moment and using the stolen ultimate before Sora forces her out.

proof.summary: Sora's Shifting Skies enables his Thunder or Torrent form power spike, while Valentina can steal an enemy ultimate and cast it, letting her contest Sora by copying his core form-switching tool.
worksBestWhen: Valentina copies Sora before the decisive fight.
Valentina has enough space to use the stolen form without being instantly collapsed on.
Valentina's team plays around the stolen ultimate window instead of treating it as a simple lockdown tool.
failureCases: Sora bursts or forces Valentina out before she can use the stolen ultimate.
The exact copied Sora behavior changes or requires further in-game validation.

reasons: Minotaur can punish Sora after Sora commits into close range, using airborne crowd control to interrupt Sora's follow-up window.
Sora depends on mobility, form-based pressure, and continued action after entry; Minotaur's teamfight control can stop that sequence before Sora fully converts the engage into damage.
Minotaur also provides frontline protection and healing support, which helps reduce Sora's value when Sora tries to dive the backline during grouped fights.

proof.summary: Sora wants to enter with mobility and keep fighting through Thunder or Torrent follow-up, while Minotaur can answer the commit with airborne crowd control, frontline protection, and teamfight healing support.
worksBestWhen: Minotaur saves his crowd control for Sora's real entry instead of using it too early.
Minotaur stays near the backline or objective area where Sora wants to dive.
The team follows up while Sora is airborne or forced to stop his combo.
failureCases: Sora baits Minotaur's ultimate before committing.
Sora uses Thunder untargetable timing to avoid the first control window.
Minotaur is too far from the target Sora is diving.
Sora plays around Minotaur by split-pushing or forcing smaller skirmishes instead of grouped fights.
